question: State and explain the necessary and sufficient conditions for a connected graph to have an Euler circuit.
expectedPoints: A connected graph has an Euler circuit iff every vertex has even degree
Explain that all vertices must have even degree for closed traversal
Mention graph must be connected

question: State and explain the necessary and sufficient conditions for a connected graph to have an Euler path but not an Euler circuit.
expectedPoints: A connected graph has an Euler path iff exactly two vertices have odd degree
Start at one odd degree vertex, end at the other odd degree vertex
Graph must be connected

question: Explain the Seven Bridges of Königsberg problem and its significance in graph theory.
expectedPoints: Problem: Walk through city crossing each of 7 bridges exactly once
Euler proved it impossible in 1736
This gave birth to graph theory as a mathematical discipline

question: Differentiate between Fleury's algorithm and Hierholzer's algorithm for finding Euler paths/circuits.
expectedPoints: Fleury's: Simple but O(|E|²), avoids bridges unless necessary
Hierholzer's: Efficient O(|E|), builds and splices cycles
Hierholzer is preferred for large graphs

question: Define: (i) Euler path (ii) Euler circuit (iii) Bridge in a graph
expectedPoints: Euler path: Trail visiting every edge exactly once, starts and ends at different vertices
Euler circuit: Closed trail visiting every edge exactly once, returns to start
Bridge: Edge whose removal disconnects the graph

question: What is a Semi-Eulerian graph? Give an example and state its properties.
expectedPoints: Semi-Eulerian graph has Euler path but not Euler circuit
Exactly two vertices have odd degree
Example: A graph with 4 vertices where two have degree 3 and two have degree 1

question: Apply Fleury's algorithm to find an Euler path in the Königsberg bridge graph.
expectedPoints: The graph has 4 vertices all with odd degree (3,3,3,3)
Since 4 odd vertices, no Euler path exists
This is why Euler proved the problem impossible

question: Find whether the following graph has an Euler circuit, Euler path, or neither. Justify your answer with degree analysis: Graph with vertices A,B,C,D and edges AB, BC, CD, DA, AC.
expectedPoints: Calculate degrees: A(3), B(2), C(3), D(2)
Vertices A and C have odd degree (3 each)
Since exactly 2 vertices have odd degree, it has an Euler path but not Euler circuit
Path starts at A or C and ends at the other

question: Explain Fleury's algorithm step-by-step with an example. Analyze its time complexity.
expectedPoints: Step 1: Start from any vertex if Euler circuit, or odd-degree vertex if Euler path
Step 2: At each step, choose non-bridge edge; if only bridge available, use it
Step 3: Remove edge after traversal
Continue until all edges used
Time complexity: O(|E|²) due to bridge detection at each step

question: Explain Hierholzer's algorithm for finding an Euler circuit. Illustrate with a suitable graph.
expectedPoints: Start at any vertex, keep traversing until returning to start forming a cycle
If unvisited edges remain, start new cycle from vertex in existing cycle
Splice new cycle into existing circuit at that vertex
Repeat until all edges visited
Time complexity: O(|E|)

question: Draw a graph with 5 vertices having an Euler circuit. Verify the condition and give one Euler circuit for the graph.
expectedPoints: Draw connected graph where all vertices have even degree
Example: Cycle C5 or add diagonals to make all degrees even
Verify all degrees are even (2 or 4)
Provide actual Euler circuit listing vertices in order
